data.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum, Dogecoin prices slide due to inflation concerns. Ethereum dips to $3,680.37 after hitting $4,000 peak.
data.description: Leading cryptocurrencies, including Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Dogecoin, experienced a decline along with stocks due to a rise in consumer prices leading to fears of no interest rate cuts. Prices at 9:40 p.m. ET showed Bitcoin down by 2.17% at $115,531.73, Ethereum down by 3.77% at $3,680.37, and Dogecoin down by 5.72% at $0.2084. Bitcoin hit an intraday low of $114,369.87, causing a market-wide decrease. Long traders suffered losses as over $570 million in long positions were wiped out in 24 hours. Despite this decline, Bitcoin and Ethereum had profitable July with Bitcoin gaining nearly 8% and Ethereum soaring over 48%. Bitcoin's open interest fell by 0.86%, and Ethereum saw a 1.83% drop in unsettled contract funds. The Crypto Fear & Greed Index indicated weakening "greed" sentiment in the market. In the past 24 hours, top gainers were Toncoin with +2.00% at $3.49, XDC Network with +1.77% at $0.09785, and Story (IP) with +1.16% at $6.02. The global cryptocurrency market capitalization was at a steady level.

data.title: GenScript Biotech's MSCI ESG rating upgraded to AA, a global leader in life science with HK.1548 code.
data.description: On July 31, 2025, GenScript Biotech Corporation (HK.1548), a prominent global company offering life science research and manufacturing services, revealed that their Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) rating ascended from A to AA. This upgrade was issued by MSCI in acknowledgment of the company's exceptional performance.

data.title: Mission Valley Bancorp reports Q2 2025 net income of $1.2 million or $0.37 per diluted share, down from $2.0 million last year.
data.description: In SUN VALLEY, California, Mission Valley Bancorp (OTCQX: MVLY) disclosed its financial results for Q2 2025, reporting a net income of $1.2 million, translating to $0.37 per diluted share. This marks a decrease compared to the net income of $2.0 million and $0.60 per diluted share in the same quarter last year.
